Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **V = length × width × depth**. Enter your measurements and get results instantly. Calculate how much soil you need for rectangular raised beds.
useCases: Calculating soil volume for raised garden beds
seoKeywords: how much soil for raised beds
explanation:
**Raised bed gardening** combines soil science, plant biology, and climate management. Proper soil volume ensures healthy plant growth and efficient water management.

## How to Calculate

This calculator applies the volume formula directly to your measurements. Simply enter the length, width, and depth of your raised bed to get the volume in both cubic meters and liters. The tool checks for typical values and alerts if entries seem unusual.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference tool. For critical decisions (large landscaping projects, commercial operations), consult with a soil scientist or landscaping professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How deep should my raised bed be?
faq.a: Most vegetables need 6-12 inches (15-30 cm). Deeper beds allow better root development for larger plants like tomatoes and carrots.

faq.q: What type of soil should I use?
faq.a: Use a mix of topsoil, compost, and peat moss or coconut coir (about 40% compost for best results). Avoid garden soil alone—it compacts.

faq.q: Should I fill the bed completely to the top?
faq.a: Leave 2-3 inches (5-7 cm) of space at the top for watering. Overfilling leads to water runoff and soil erosion.

faq.q: Can I use this calculator for circular or oddly-shaped beds?
faq.a: This calculator works for rectangular beds. For circular beds, use Area = π × r², then multiply by depth.

faq.q: Should I add drainage material at the bottom?
faq.a: Yes, 2 inches (5 cm) of gravel or hardware cloth at the base improves drainage and prevents pest problems.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
Very accurate for standard raised beds. We use internationally validated formulas. For large landscaping projects, verify measurements twice.

faq.q: Is my data stored or tracked?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't collect or send data to any server.
